commands.review_summary:
The branch changes `src/commands/doctor-state-integrity.test.ts` to replace a Windows-only skip with a directory-symlink capability probe and use Windows junctions for the symlinked state-dir regression test.

PR surface: Tests +15. Total +15 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. for the coverage gap: current main source shows the relevant symlink regression test is skipped on every Windows run. No runtime user bug reproduction is needed because this PR is test-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] No repair job is needed; the PR is small, proof is now sufficient, and routine CI plus maintainer review should gate merge.

target.title: test: make doctor state integrity symlink test robust on Windows
target.body:
Replaces the hardcoded Windows skip with a dynamic directory symlink capability check. If directory symlinks are supported (either on POSIX or Windows with junctions), the test runs. This enables test execution on Windows when permissions are available.

### Verified Windows Proof

The following test execution log shows the test run on Windows. All 30 tests in the suite (including the dynamically skipped/run symlinked path test) passed successfully:

```
 RUN  v4.1.7 C:/Users/ANIRUDDHA/.gemini/antigravity/scratch/openclaw

 ✓  commands  ../../src/commands/doctor-state-integrity.test.ts (30 tests) 4354ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  30 passed (30)
```
target.author: aniruddhaadak80
